goal: Generate production-ready place-specific set files with a binding 6x7 structure, where place logic governs sets 1, 2 and 4, emne logic governs sets 3 and 5, and concept logic governs set 6.
core_principle: place -> set_mode -> stedskarakter -> spørsmålsvinkel -> konkret spørsmål; emner are support in sets 1,2,4 and drivers only in sets 3,5,6.
blocked_formulations: Hvorfor er dette stedet relevant for
Hva gjør stedet til et eksempel på
Hva gjør dette stedet relevant for
Hvordan fungerer stedet som eksempel på

gjenkjenning.purpose: Identify the place through a concrete signature trait.
good_openings: Hva ved stedet gjør det lett å kjenne igjen
Hva er det første som skiller
knowledge_requirements: must name a concrete place trait
must add why the trait matters in the city

historisk_endring.purpose: Frame the place through change over time.
knowledge_requirements: must mention at least one dated or periodized shift
must connect shift to current place character

teknisk_fysisk.purpose: Ask about construction, infrastructure, material or physical solution.
knowledge_requirements: must include a concrete technical or material detail
must explain what that detail enables

bruk.purpose: Ask about actual use, rhythms and user groups.
good_openings: Hva brukes stedet til i dag

romlig_lesning.purpose: Read movement, openness, closure, direction and position in space.
good_openings: Hva gjør at stedet åpner seg
knowledge_requirements: must tie it to movement or orientation

saertrekk.purpose: Distinguish the place from similar places in Oslo.
knowledge_requirements: must not fall back to generic category labels

kontrast.purpose: Use contrast against a nearby or comparable place.
good_openings: Hva er den tydeligste forskjellen mellom
Hvilket trekk gjør at dette ikke fungerer som
knowledge_requirements: must compare against an explicit contrast target or same-type place
must explain difference, not just name it

tidslag.purpose: Read multiple periods or surviving traces in the same place.
good_openings: Hvilke epoker kan fortsatt leses her
knowledge_requirements: must identify at least one surviving trace
must connect trace to present-day reading

begrepsdefinisjon.purpose: Define or identify a core concept that actually helps read the place.

begrepskontrast.purpose: Distinguish between two close concepts, terms or analytical pairs.

begrepsanvendelse.purpose: Apply a concept to the place instead of only defining it abstractly.

set_specific_rules.1: Knowledge should explain the concrete fact and why it matters for reading the place.
set_specific_rules.2: Knowledge should connect detail/history/architecture to how the place functions.
set_specific_rules.3: Knowledge should anchor terminology in the place and not stay generic.
set_specific_rules.4: Knowledge should deepen the place story through institutions, functions or development.
set_specific_rules.5: Knowledge should show analytical reading, not just terminology.
set_specific_rules.6: Knowledge should define the concept clearly and show why it helps interpret this place.

output_mapping.dimension: Derived from angle_rules.dimension_hints and/or emne dimensions.
output_mapping.topic: Concrete place topic in sets 1,2,4; faglig topic in sets 3,5,6.
output_mapping.emne_id: Optional support field in sets 1,2,4; required and load-bearing in sets 3,5,6.
output_mapping.related_emner: Fill from emne relations and mapping context, especially in sets 3,5,6.
output_mapping.core_concepts: Required in sets 3,5,6 when concept-bearing emner are used.
output_mapping.concept_focus: Required in set 6; optional but useful in sets 3 and 5.
output_mapping.tags: Use place_type, subtype, set mode and major thematic markers.
output_mapping.question_scope: place for sets 1,2,4; emne for sets 3,5; concept for set 6.
output_mapping.difficulty: Low in set 1, mixed in sets 2-5, conceptually hardest in set 6.
output_mapping.question_type: fact/use/analysis/comparison in sets 1-5; concept/definition/concept_application in set 6.
output_mapping.source: Must list the concrete sources, files or place/emne evidence used to draft the question.

park.extra_rule: Do not let two parks in the same batch share the same subtype+primary family combination.

knutepunkt.extra_rule: Force at least one question on flows, transfers or waiting.

gate.extra_rule: Prefer street character, commerce, rhythm or transformation over generic byrom wording.

boligomrade.extra_rule: At least one question should distinguish the area from another housing type in Oslo.

plass.extra_rule: At least one question should address the place as a scene, threshold or civic room.

omrade.extra_rule: Prefer large-scale area logic over single-building detail.

generator_pipeline: 1. Read place, place.quiz_profile, emne mappings, emnemapping and relevant prior quiz context.
2. Build a six-set plan using binding_set_structure and set_mode_rules.
3. For sets 1, 2 and 4: collect concrete place facts, events, functions, institutions, persons, development lines and signature traits before consulting emner.
4. For sets 3 and 5: choose emner from place.emne_ids through quiz_profile angles; emner are now allowed to drive the question.
5. For set 6: extract and rank core concepts, concept_focus terms and definitional pairs from mapped emner and prior knowledge fields.
6. Select question families allowed for the current set mode and distribute them to avoid repetition.
7. Bind each draft question to at least one concrete signature_feature or must_include item; in sets 3,5,6 also bind to emne_id/core_concepts.
8. Draft question text using preferred_openings and reject blocked_formulations.
9. Generate three plausible options with one correct answer; ensure distractors are close enough to be fair.
10. Write knowledge text that adds context beyond the answer and follows set-specific knowledge rules.
11. Fill the full output contract including quiz_id, question_scope, answerIndex, difficulty, question_type, emne fields, tags and source.
12. Validate full file shape: exactly 6 sets, exactly 7 questions in each set, correct set modes, no duplicate or near-duplicate questions, and correct manifest pointers.
validator_checks: Sets 1, 2 and 4 are place-driven and not emne-driven.
No blocked formulation in question text.
Question text must mention or clearly hinge on a place-specific trait in all sets.
No more than one question per emne-angle pair per set unless set 6 explicitly compares concepts.
At least three distinct question families in each set except set 6, which may use concept families but must still vary its concept task types.
At least two signature_features or must_include items represented in each set.
If place has contrast_targets, at least one comparison question across the full place quiz.
Set 6 must use at least four distinct core concepts or concept pairs across the seven questions.
No question should be a thin rewording of another question in the same file.
Options must be plausible and answerIndex must match answer.
Manifest must point to each set individually.

binding_set_structure.binding_rule: Only sets 3, 5 and 6 may be emne-/begrepsdriven. Sets 1, 2 and 4 must be built from concrete place-specific information first.

2.description: Still place-near, but with more history, architecture and detailed use.

4.description: Return to the place, but with more demanding concrete logic.

5.description: Second faglig set with deeper structure and analysis.
must_prioritize: terminologi fra emner og tidligere quiz
begreper som faktisk hjelper spilleren å lese stedet

sets_1_2_4.rule: emne_id, related_emner, core_concepts and concept_focus may be present, but the question must originate from place-specific facts, traits, functions, institutions or concrete history.

sets_3_5_6.rule: emne_id, related_emner, core_concepts and concept_focus must be meaningfully populated and should actively structure the question.

authoring_workflow: Build sets 1, 2 and 4 from concrete place facts first.
Then check emnekart, emnemapping and relevant emner.
Build sets 3 and 5 from the strongest mapped emner.
Build set 6 as a pure concept set from core_concepts, concept_focus, topic and knowledge.
Run duplicate and quality validation across all 42 questions.
Emit manifest entries for all six sets.
